food_name: Fish Cake Noodle Soup (Bún Chả Cá)

ontology.foodex2_description: Noodle soup — composite dish with fish and vegetables
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (soups and broths, composite) used as the closest defensible anchor for this Vietnamese fish cake noodle soup. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for Bún chả cá.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 500 g (one bowl), providing roughly 450 kcal, 28 g protein, 12 g fat, and 55 g carbohydrate, with 1800 mg sodium from the broth and fish cakes.

nutrient_source.title: FAO/INFOODS Food Composition Table for Use in East Asia and Southeast Asia — composite dish estimation for Vietnamese fish cake noodle soup
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Bún chả cá as a composite dish. Values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table component data for rice vermicelli, fish cake (steamed/boiled), fish broth, and fresh vegetables, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients (rice noodles USDA #20066, fish cake analogue USDA #15264, vegetable broth components). The input record's per-100g values (energy 90 kcal, protein 5.6 g, fat 2.4 g, carbohydrate 11.0 g) are consistent with this component-based estimation and are retained with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — rice vermicelli / rice noodle analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bún chả cá as a composite dish. GI estimated at 58 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source: rice vermicelli noodles (bún), for which the University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values of 53–61 (mean ~58) for boiled rice noodles/vermicelli. The protein from fish cakes (5.6 g/100g) and dietary fibre (0.8 g/100g) from vegetables are expected to modestly attenuate the glycaemic response. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (58 × 55) / 100 = 31.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 360 mg/100g; a 500 g bowl delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium, representing 78–90% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced fish sauce and low-sodium broth, limit fish cake portions (a primary sodium source), and avoid consuming the full soup broth to substantially reduce sodium intake per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 11.0 g/100g from rice vermicelli (GI ~58, medium); a 500 g bowl delivers approximately 55 g carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~32.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should control portion size to limit carbohydrate load, retain bean sprouts and fresh herbs to maximise fibre intake and attenuate glycaemic response, and monitor post-meal blood glucose when consuming a full bowl.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 360 mg/100g (1800 mg per bowl) is the primary cardiovascular concern; saturated fat is low at 0.6 g/100g and trans fat is negligible at 0.02 g/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should prioritise sodium reduction by requesting less fish sauce and avoiding the broth, while the low saturated fat profile and fish-based protein are otherwise compatible with a heart-healthy diet when portion size is controlled.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Boiled preparation minimises AGE formation; anti-inflammatory herbs (dill, green onions) and low saturated fat support RA suitability.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis and nightshade sensitivity should reduce or omit tomatoes; choose freshly made fish cakes over processed varieties to minimise additives and sodium.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bún chả cá (Fish Cake Noodle Soup) is a Vietnamese noodle soup dish widely served in Vietnam and at Vietnamese food stalls in Singapore. The dish consists of thin rice vermicelli noodles (bún) served in a clear, savoury fish-based broth, topped with pan-fried or steamed fish cakes (chả cá), fresh dill, green onions, tomatoes, bean sprouts, and lime. The broth is typically seasoned with fish sauce, shrimp paste, and aromatics, giving it a deeply umami, mildly pungent flavour. The texture profile combines soft, slippery noodles with springy, lightly chewy fish cakes and crisp fresh vegetables. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 90 kcal, 5.6 g protein, 2.4 g fat (0.6 g saturated), and 11.0 g carbohydrate, with 360 mg sodium — the sodium content is the primary clinical concern, particularly for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients, as a standard 500 g bowl delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 58 (medium) based on the rice vermicelli base, with a glycemic load of approximately 32 per serving. The dish is dairy-free and generally halal. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or lunch meal in Vietnamese culinary tradition and is available at Vietnamese restaurants and hawker stalls across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Bún chả cá as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table component data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy ingredient entries. The input per-100g values are consistent with component-based estimation and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate based on rice vermicelli data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation basis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Vietnamese noodle soup dish is not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not reported in the approved sources consulted. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite dish are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA FoodData Central at the composite dish level as of 2026. All core macros (protein_g 5.6, fat_g 2.4, carbohydrate_g 11.0) are non-zero and consistent with a fish-and-noodle soup dish; no zero-macro justification is required. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.02 g/100g (trace level from fish cake processing), retained from input. The dish is listed with country_of_origin as Singapore in the input, reflecting the serving context; the culinary origin is Vietnam.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as per G5 hard gate requirements.

Added mandatory local names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to local_names and aliases_by_language, and ensured they are present in the flat aliases array, addressing G6 and Q1/Q8 failures. This is because the dish is served in Singapore, which requires these languages.
Ensured consistency between aliases_by_language and the flat aliases array by adding new local names to the flat aliases array.
